scene.scenePrompt: Wide establishing shot, a cluttered and dimly lit room at night, summer atmosphere. Stacks of old boxes, scattered papers, a vintage tape recorder on a small table, tangled cables on the floor, a single bare bulb casting weak yellow light. The walls are peeling, revealing faint outlines of a cityscape beneath. Through a cracked window, the distant glow of neon signs pulses in the humid air. The space feels claustrophobic yet dreamlike, with a subtle warp effect beginning to distort the edges of the room, blending into a surreal geometric grid floor pattern in purple and pink hues. Muted warm shadows contrast with cool neon hints, creating a tense, atmospheric mood of impending temporal shift.

beats.narration: Sweat clings to your neck on this summer night as you finally shove the last box of old belongings into the corner. That cassette tape you scavenged from the vaporwave thrift store has a faded plastic shell, looking like an old, weathered scar.
With a sharp *click*, the vintage player swallows the tape. The gears turn, and the hissing static sounds like a soft sigh. Then—without warning, a young girl's singing pierces the silence.
The singing is clear and full of life, completely different from the weak humming of the mother in your memories. The lyrics are muffled, but they suddenly become crystal clear during the chorus—
"...The summer night breeze cannot reach tomorrow... Xiaoyuan, don't look back..."

beats.speaker: You
beats.line: Xiaoyuan... Is she calling my name?

beats.narration: Your heart pounds wildly. The singing doesn't stop, but the entire room begins to tremble slightly. The old posters on the wall curl and peel away, revealing shifting, neon-pink spots of light beneath.

The spots of light spread rapidly, swallowing the clutter, swallowing the walls. You watch as the Chinese characters on neon signs flicker and reorganize in mid-air—'Video Hall,' 'Disco.' A massive suction force erupts from the source of the sound.

effect.nextSceneSeed: On a neon-lit street in 1988, you collapse onto the sidewalk, the cassette tape clenched tightly in your palm, surrounded by strangers from a bygone era.

choices.label: Try to turn off the player

effect.nextSceneSeed: Inside the temporal vortex, neon lights and shadows flash past like streams of data as you fall toward the sky of 1988.

storyState.logline: After unexpectedly hearing the swan song of your late mother's youth, you attempt to travel through time to change her tragic fate, only to discover she buried a forbidden romance for your sake—one that could completely erase your very existence.
storyState.genreTags: Time Travel / Romantic Mystery / Vaporwave Aesthetic / Drama-style Twists
storyState.protagonist: You, a young girl who has just lost her mother, scavenged a mysterious cassette tape from a vaporwave-aesthetic thrift store. Upon playing it, you unexpectedly hear your mother's singing from her youth. You long to go back in time to change her fate of dying young, but deep down, you fear uncovering a past she never mentioned—and dread even more that this truth might shake the very foundation of your existence. You are trapped in an immediate conflict between 'wanting to save your mother' and 'fearing the truth.'
storyState.castNotes:
Lin Yuetang (Mother): A rebellious 1980s teenager whose singing is full of vitality, though she secretly carries an unspeakable forbidden love and family secrets. You will meet her after traveling back, but you cannot reveal your identity as her daughter.
Shen Mo: A mysterious transfer student from the 1980s and Lin Yuetang's secret lover. Seemingly aloof but deeply devoted, his existence might be the key to changing everything, or the root of all tragedy.
Old Chen: An 1980s video rental shop owner who knows many secrets of the past. He is your only potential ally after you travel back, but he seems to have some connection to Lin Yuetang's death.
storyState.synopsis: On a summer night, the protagonist plays an old cassette tape she bought, only to hear the singing of her late mother, Lin Yuetang, in her youth—with the lyrics directly calling out her name, 'Xiaoyuan.' The song triggers a temporal distortion, turning the room into a neon vortex that drags the protagonist into the year 1988.
openThreads: Why the cassette tape sings the protagonist's name, 'Xiaoyuan'
The warning behind 'don't look back' in the mother's song
The mechanics and cost of time travel
relationships: Lin Yuetang (Recording): Her youthful voice is full of unknown vitality and secrets, serving as your only 'connection' before traveling back.
storyState.nextHook: Next Scene: The time travel is complete. The protagonist appears on the streets of 1988, needing to assess her situation and search for the source of the singing or any clues.

worldSetting:
This is an AI interactive story game targeted at a 【Female-oriented】 audience, featuring intense dramatic conflicts and rapid plot twists in the style of short-form web dramas.
Story Style: Multi-branching twists. Pacing: Fast-paced and satisfying.
Opening setup for the featured story 'Summer Nostalgia Love Letter': She buys an 80s cassette tape at a thrift store. When she plays it, she unexpectedly hears the voice of her late mother in her youth. Guided by the voice, she travels back in time to her mother's youth, attempting to change her mother's fate of dying young, only to uncover a forbidden love her mother never spoke of.
Please dive straight into this opening to give the player a strong sense of immersion and instant gratification. Subsequent branches should maintain the high twist density of short-form dramas, ensuring players see immediate consequences for every choice.
styleGuide: Vaporwave aesthetic, vertical composition. A gorgeous, highly stylized character with purple hair and striking facial contours, looking over lowered sunglasses in a retro summer setting. Geometric pink grid floor, palm trees, purple ocean sunset. Glitch filters, retro neon pink and cyan palette.
